mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power, which depresses aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 8.6% indicates elevated unemployment
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative UNRATE changes fail to predict negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% month-over-month, signaling weakening consumer demand and inventory accumulation risk. This triggers a 5-trading-day negative return signal as equity markets reprice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeds 2.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) year-over-year growth remains above 3%, the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy. This dual condition compresses equity valuations and produces negative 60-day forward returns as real rates remain elevated.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 0.08% and CPIAUCSL at 226.763
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (joint condition) shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) relative to single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month, indicating supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization. This signals margin compression and lower corporate profitability, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes fail to predict negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) both decline simultaneously, indicating synchronized weakness across consumer and residential investment demand. This joint deterioration signals broad-based demand destruction, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month decline > 5% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth < 1%
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 628.0 thousand units, RSAFS at 399349.0 million dollars
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of jointly triggered instances, or the interaction effect shows no incremental predictive power (R² improvement < 0.02) over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 50,000 persons per month, signaling labor market softening and reduced hiring momentum. This triggers a 60-day negative return signal as markets anticipate downstream consumption weakness.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) declines by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month window, signaling monetary easing and reduced discount rates on equity cash flows. This produces positive 20-day forward returns as the easing cycle begins.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 0.08% indicates near-zero rates
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle or policy pivot
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 3% annualized while unemployment (UNRATE) falls below 4%, indicating a strong late-cycle expansion. This dual condition produces positive 60-day forward returns as earnings growth accelerates and recession risk remains low.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 13337.8 billion chained 2017 dollars, UNRATE at 8.6%
regime_hint: strong expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of jointly triggered instances, or the interaction effect shows no incremental predictive power (R² improvement < 0.02) over single-series models.
